on these occasions In ancient times kings had human observatories.
Without meticulous care and keen observation, how could they find
out if Jupiter became visible at daytime or at night (VIII. 18).
Geographical details ofVaraha do not depart much from those
of his predecessors. He follows Vrddha Garga in this field. Geogra-
phical details are met with even in Vedic literature: The Satqpatha
Brdhmana states that the land known, as Kuru-Pdhcalas was the home

Introduction xix
and centre of Brahminical culture. It speaks of the Madkyadeta,
Kosald) Videha, Kali, Gandhdras, Salvos and Kekayas. The Kum-Pdncdla
country was comprised of Kosala, Vide has, Sivaknas, and Srhjayas.
The Aitareya Brdhmana speaks of the Kuru-Pdhcdla country as com-
prising the Vaias and UHnaras. To the south of this Middle Country
